title: Repair Cliff Vesting Router With CPI Claims
summary: Repair a partially working vesting router that controls token payouts through a helper vault program via CPI.
businessLogic: An admin initializes a vesting stream for one beneficiary, one mint, a cliff round, and a total number of rounds.
A helper guarded-vault program holds custody, and the router program must release vested funds through CPI using its authority PDA.
The admin funds the full stream once and advances rounds over time, while the beneficiary may claim only the newly vested delta.

instructions.name: initialize_stream
instructions.description: Create stream state, derive the router authority PDA, and CPI into the guarded-vault helper to create the custody vault.

instructions.name: fund
instructions.description: Let the admin fund the stream exactly once by transferring the full vesting amount into the helper vault.

instructions.name: advance_round
instructions.description: Let only the admin advance the stream's current round monotonically up to the configured total number of rounds.

instructions.name: claim
instructions.description: Let only the configured beneficiary claim the newly vested delta into a token account they actually own, using a CPI release from the helper vault.

invariants: Only the configured admin may fund the stream or advance rounds.
Only the configured beneficiary may claim from the stream.
Repeated claims across rounds must release only the newly vested delta, never the full vested amount again.
Claims must go through the guarded-vault helper via CPI using the router authority PDA.
Claim payouts must go only to a token account owned by the beneficiary and using the configured mint.
failureConditions: Paying the full vested amount on every claim instead of subtracting already claimed amounts.
Allowing non-admin signers to advance rounds or fund the stream.
Allowing a non-beneficiary signer to claim from the stream.
Allowing the beneficiary to redirect a valid claim into an attacker-owned token account.
